name: Kale

pros.title: micronutrient density is ridiculous
pros.explanation: This is the kind of vegetable profile that can clearly carry a category.

pros.title: brings a strong cruciferous plant-compound angle
pros.explanation: Kale offers more than just raw vitamin numbers and has a real phytochemical identity.

pros.title: very low-downside whole food
pros.explanation: It gives a lot back nutritionally without bringing much obvious baggage.

cons.title: prep and texture can be a barrier
cons.explanation: Kale often asks more from the eater than softer, easier vegetables do.

cons.title: its flavour is not equally easy for everyone
cons.explanation: That practical friction matters even when the nutrient profile is excellent.

cons.title: very high vitamin K can matter in specific medication contexts
cons.explanation: That is not a general downside, but it is a more useful caveat than generic vegetable filler.

sourceNotes: Expanded dataset sample for FoodRanked pressure-testing.
Values are approximate representative placeholders for category tuning, not a clinical nutrient database.
